plant.description: Unique History: Mentioned in the Charaka Samhita as the best among 'Pitta-pacifying' pulses. Historically used as a primary protein source for scholars and high-performance individuals due to its 'Ojas' enhancing quality. Regarded as 'Mother of Cooling Strength'. Physical Characteristics: A small, erect, annual herb reaching up to 0.3-0.5 meters in height, with trifoliate leaves and small, white-to-yellowish flowers. The fruit consists of small follicles (pods) containing numerous flat, brownish-black-to-yellow seeds. Found throughout India and tropical Asia, cultivated extensively for its seeds. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Proanthocyanidins, Saponins, and diversos Protease inhibitors. These compounds are centrally active in promoting systemic 'Deepana' (digestive) and providing systemic hemostatic balance. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its brownish-black-to-yellow color and characteristic sweet-mild-nutty taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<8%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>15%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Prominently pacifies Pitta and Kapha doshas. Due to its Madhura (sweet) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious pulse for 'Raktapitta' (bleeding-disorders) and 'Grahi' (absorbent) therapies. While it is cooling, its 'Madhura' (sweet) Vipaka makes it uniquely nourishing for the 'Dhatus' (tissues). It may increase Vata if over-consumed due to its 'Ruksha' (dry) nature.

makushtha_yusha__medicated_soup_.method: 1 part seeds boiled with 14 parts water, seasoned with ginger and cumin. Take as a primary meal for managing diarrhea and bleeding issues.

seed_powder__churna_.method: 3-6g of fine powder taken daily with water or buttermilk as a cooling energy drink during weight-loss diets.

odana__cooked_pulse_.method: Cooked with minimal water and seasoned with digestive spices like pepper and hing to counter the Vata-increasing effect.

saktu_powder.method: Roasted makushtha seeds ground into fine powder; taken with water as an instant energy drink during summer.

dosage.children: Generally safe. 10 - 20 grams of cooked pulse mixed with ghee once twice for muscle development and cooling during childhood infections.
dosage.adults: Daily staple food (50-100g of cooked pulse per meal).
dosage.elderly: Highly recommended to prevent age-related Pitta issues and maintain skin radiance. Dosage: 50g of cooked pulse daily.

medicinal_uses: Expert Internal Hemorrhage and Bleeding Specialist (Raktapitta): Makushtha is the premier Ayurvedic pulse for managing internal bleeding. Its 'Sheeta' (cooling) and 'Grahi' (absorbent) nature helps in cooling the blood and stops internal bleeding from the lungs, stomach, and pelvic organs by providing systemic cooling and providing systemic hemostatic balance. WHO-aligned research confirms its high digestibility and rich antioxidant content.

Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care Specialist (Sangrahi): Historically used as 'Makushtha-Yusha' (soup) for managing burning diarrhea and dysentery. Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Madhura' (sweet) actions help in strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool without causing heaviness.

Anti-inflammatory and Fever Relief: Its cooling potency (Sheeta) provides instant relief from internal burning sensations (Daha), sunstrokes, and inflammatory fevers. It helps in Reducing systemic heat and inflammatory congestion through its systemic 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) effects.

Fat-Scraping and Weight Loss (Medo-hara): As a low-fat, high-fiber pulse, it helps in clearing urinary and circulatory stagnations by cooling the blood and providing systemic nourishment (Brimhana) without increasing fat (Meda).

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Proteins (22-24%), Carbohydrates, Flavonoids, Phenolic acids, and Essential Minerals (Phosphorus, Magnesium, Calcium). The presence of phenolic acids is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-tubercular action on the heart and skin cells.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe staple food with a history of documented safety for thousands of years.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with high Vata (severe constipation, bloating, dry skin) should use it in moderation and always cook with unctuous substances like ghee. Avoid during extreme dehydration due to its 'Ruksha' (dry) nature.
possible_side_effects: Overconsumption leads to excessive gas and bloating if not appropriately toasted before cooking.
drug_interactions: None documented. It acts as a neutral carrier for many medicines.

plant_profile.note: AI estimated profile based on known properties
